summary: Verified medication alarm optional feature implementation. All 11 backend/model tests passed. Frontend code review confirms alarm toggle exists with correct data-testid='alarm-toggle', wrapped in Capacitor.isNativePlatform() check (only visible on native apps). notificationManager defaults areLocalAlarmsEnabled to false. App.js syncs alarm_enabled from backend to localStorage on init.

details: ✅ Endpoint exists and returns 401 without auth (expected)
✅ Handles existing records without alarm_enabled by defaulting to false
✅ Endpoint exists and returns 401 without auth (expected)
✅ Uses upsert to handle users without existing settings
✅ alarm_enabled field exists with default False
✅ Wrapped in Capacitor.isNativePlatform() check (only visible on native)
✅ Loads setting from backend on mount via getNotificationSettings
✅ Returns false when setting is null (default to disabled)
✅ Only schedules local alarms when enabled
✅ Syncs alarm_enabled from backend to localStorage on init

findings: ✅ Handles legacy records by adding alarm_enabled=False if missing
✅ Alarm toggle wrapped in Capacitor.isNativePlatform() check
✅ Loads setting from backend on mount (lines 139-156)
✅ Saves setting to backend on toggle (lines 159-196)
✅ areLocalAlarmsEnabled() returns false when setting is null
✅ Syncs alarm_enabled from backend to localStorage on native platform

test_credentials.note: Google OAuth required for full end-to-end testing. Alarm toggle only visible on native (Capacitor) platform.

context_for_next_testing_agent: Medication alarm feature is fully implemented. The alarm toggle in ProfilePage is wrapped in Capacitor.isNativePlatform() check, so it won't appear in browser screenshots - this is by design. To test the toggle UI, you would need to run the app on a native device (Android/iOS). Backend API tests confirm the alarm_enabled field is properly handled in GET/PUT /api/settings/notifications endpoints. The notificationManager correctly defaults areLocalAlarmsEnabled to false, and App.js syncs the setting from backend to localStorage on app init.
